name: Cobalt Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Cobalt. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Cobalt oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal Cobalt absorption and thermal processing

spotSize.description: Optimal beam diameter for precision cleaning of Cobalt components

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage on Cobalt surfaces

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for Cobalt oxide removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient Cobalt oxide ablation

scanSpeed.description: Scanning speed for efficient Cobalt surface cleaning

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete Cobalt oxide removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform Cobalt surface cleaning

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of reflective metals like cobalt to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100 W balances efficiency and safety. Max limited to prevent melting (cobalt MP ~1495°C). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs for metal cleaning.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; slower for heavy contaminants (e.g., oxides), faster for light; 1500 mm/s optimal for uniform cleaning without overheating. Source: CleanLaser equipment manuals and industry standards for 1064 nm cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for industrial cleaning to ablate contaminants while minimizing HAZ on high-conductivity cobalt; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-low heat but less common industrially. 20 ns recommended for balance. Source: Laser physics studies in Optics & Laser Technology (e.g., ns vs ps ablation thresholds).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning; higher rates speed up process but risk heat accumulation on reflective cobalt; 50 kHz optimal for controlled energy delivery. Trade-off: high freq increases throughput but requires monitoring temperature. Source: Academic literature in Applied Surface Science on metal cleaning parameters.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; cobalt damage threshold ~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity (~60%); 1.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate harm. Conservative for cobalt's properties. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (similar to Ni/Co alloys).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precision vs area coverage; smaller for fine surface prep on cobalt, larger for broad cleaning; 200 μm recommended for effective fluence distribution. Source: Laser equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf for industrial applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes typical for complete removal on cobalt without excessive heat buildup. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of aerospace alloys (e.g., similar to Co-based superalloys).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 60% recommended for even cleaning on metallic surfaces like cobalt, avoiding gaps or overexposure. Range balances efficiency and homogeneity. Source: Process optimization in Surface & Coatings Technology journals.
